steps.title: Use the factor theorem
steps.content: If $x+2b$ is a factor, then $x=-2b$ must make $3x^2+kx+14b$ equal $0$, where $k$ is the coefficient of $x$ in the answer choice.

steps.title: Confirm by hand
steps.content: The coefficient must be $7$ more than a multiple of $6$. Among $7$, $28$, $42$, and $49$, only $49$ works because $49=6(7)+7$.

steps.title: Choose the integer case
steps.content: Choice D gives $49=6b+7$, so $b=7$, a positive integer. Then $3x^2+49x+98=(x+14)(3x+7)$, so $x+2b=x+14$ is a factor. Choice D is correct.
